author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: CLI+MCP surface for atomic bulk item mutations: JSON mutation batch on stdin over commitItemMutations

CLI command consumes a JSON array of {op:create|update|close} mutations on stdin and applies them atomically via the public SDK commitItemMutations only (no private-core imports); partial failure compensates per the SDK policy and reports per-mutation outcomes; --dry-run previews the batch; unknown ops/keys fail fast with contract-derived hints; idempotent resume via a stable --transaction-id; MCP parity tool + contracts/completions/docs/vitest wiring per the new-command checklist; command naming decided under the pm-pbyu noun-verb composition input before implementation

message: PR#616 shipped commitItemMutations (pm-y9hq) as SDK-only. Scripting agents (terminal+jq composition) and multi-agent lanes still cannot perform atomic multi-item mutations without writing TypeScript — update-many/close-many are non-transactional and pm-kipd covers only single-item stdin documents. This exposes the shipped primitive at the CLI/MCP boundary, the reverse direction of the usfg thin-layer thesis.
